title: Wildlife_test
text:
g2 <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(
    data = df_sf,
    aes(color = factor(name)),
    size = 0.75,
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "#e6194b", "#3cb44b", "#ffe119", "#4363d8", "#f58231",
    "#911eb4", "#46f0f0", "#f032e6", "#bcf60c", "#fabebe",
    "#008080", "#e6beff", "#9a6324", "#fffac8", "#800000"
  )) +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(
      override.aes = list(size = 3, alpha = 1)
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Tracking Data",
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude",
    color = "Name"
  )

g2

kaudulla_climate_monthly_clean <- read_csv("kaudulla_climate_monthly_clean.csv")

Rows: 108 Columns: 13


── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: ","
chr   (1): month_name
dbl  (11): year, month, solar_radiation, cloud_amount, soil_wetness, rainfal...
date  (1): date

ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

env_data <- kaudulla_climate_monthly_clean |>
  mutate(
    year = factor(year),

    month_name = factor(
      month_name,
      levels = c(
        "Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr",
        "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug",
        "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"
      ),
      ordered = TRUE
    )
  )


library(tidyverse)

── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
✔ purrr     1.2.2     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ plotly::filter() masks dplyr::filter(), stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()     masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors

library(plotly)

env_data2 <- env_data |>
   filter(year %in% c("2023", "2024", "2025"))

make_heatmap <- function(df, var_name, title) {

  p <- ggplot(
    df,
    aes(
      x = month_name,
      y = year,
      fill = .data[[var_name]]
    )
  ) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    scale_fill_viridis_c() +
    labs(title = title, x = "Month", y = "Year") +
    theme_minimal()

  ggplotly(p)
}
